title: CobbleStories - A Create Adventure
description: CobbleStories - A Create Adventure: is a Cobblemon modpack focused on exploration, automation, and progression built around Create.
body:
# CobbleStories - A Create Adventure

Welcome to **CobbleStories - A Create Adventure**, a Cobblemon modpack built around one idea: your Pokémon journey should feel like an adventure you build with your own hands.

This pack combines the creature-collecting, battling, and exploration of **Cobblemon** with the engineering and automation depth of **Create**. Instead of relying only on simple crafting recipes, many important parts of progression are tied into machinery, processing lines, sequenced assembly, mixing, crushing, compacting, and other Create systems.

Whether you want to complete your Pokédex, build an automated Poké Ball factory, explore for rare Pokémon, progress through quests, or set up a multiplayer world with friends, **CobbleStories** gives you a more hands-on way to play Cobblemon.

## Explore, Catch, and Train
Start your journey in a world full of Pokémon, structures, quests, resources, and progression goals. Build your team, search for rare encounters, and explore biomes to discover what each area has to offer.

- Catch and train Pokémon across your world
- Build a team for wild battles, trainer battles, raids, and progression fights
- Explore custom structures, loot locations, and world content
- Use quests and guide systems to stay grounded without feeling railroaded
- Progress at your own pace in singleplayer or multiplayer
CobbleStories is designed to feel like more than a sandbox with Pokémon added. It gives you reasons to travel, build, automate, and return stronger.

### Create-Powered Progression
Create is not just extra machinery in this pack. It is part of the main progression loop.

Many recipes have been reworked so that important Pokémon items are crafted through Create systems instead of basic crafting tables. This makes automation meaningful and gives your factories a real purpose.

You can build machines for:

- Poké Ball production
- Ancient Poké Ball production
- Healing items
- Candies and training items
- Vitamins and progression materials
- Ore processing
- Special item crafting
- Late-game Pokémon progression
Instead of crafting everything by hand, you can build working production lines that grow with your journey.

### Automation That Matters
CobbleStories rewards players who enjoy building machines with a purpose. Your Create contraptions can help support your Pokémon adventure directly.

Set up crushing lines for custom ores, automate ingredients, process materials, and build assembly systems for items you will actually use throughout the pack.

This includes support for custom processed materials such as:

- Cobalt
- Magnite
- Mythril
- Orichalcum
These resources are integrated into the pack’s crafting and automation progression, giving mining, processing, and factory building a stronger role.

### Quests, Goals, and Adventure Flow
The pack includes questing and progression systems to help guide players through the world without forcing one single playstyle.

Quests can introduce important mechanics, point you toward new goals, reward exploration, and help explain how different systems connect. You can follow the quest path, branch into automation, focus on Pokémon collection, or build a base that supports all of it.

Expect goals around:

- Early-game setup
- Create machinery
- Pokémon progression
- Exploration
- Crafting upgrades
- Resource processing
- Special items
- Long-term collection and team building

### Advanced Pokémon Mechanics
CobbleStories supports expanded Cobblemon gameplay with advanced battle and progression systems. These systems are meant to give long-term players more to work toward beyond catching a starter team and exploring.

Depending on your progression, you can work toward mechanics such as:

- Mega Evolution
- Dynamax and Gigantamax
- Legendary-focused progression
- Special battle items
- Raids and tougher encounters
- Expanded Pokémon tools and utilities
The goal is to make powerful mechanics feel earned through adventure, crafting, and progression instead of being handed out too early.

### Built for Survival and Multiplayer
CobbleStories is designed for players who want a long-term world. You can play solo, build with friends, or host a server and progress together.

The pack includes quality-of-life features, performance improvements, storage options, maps, navigation tools, quest support, and server-friendly adjustments to make longer playthroughs smoother.

Play how you want:

- Build a cozy Pokémon base
- Automate an industrial Poké Ball factory
- Travel with friends
- Hunt rare Pokémon
- Complete quests
- Prepare for late-game mechanics
- Create a server economy or shared town

### What Makes CobbleStories Different?
CobbleStories is not just Cobblemon with Create installed. The two systems are tied together.

Pokémon progression feeds into automation, and automation feeds back into your Pokémon journey. Crafting important items through machines gives your builds a real reason to exist, while exploration gives you the resources and motivation to keep expanding.

If you enjoy both Pokémon adventure and mechanical problem-solving, this pack is built for you.

Recommended Settings
This pack is made for Minecraft 1.21.1 and is intended to be played with the included mod list and resource packs.

Recommended memory:

- Minimum: **6 GB RAM**
- Recommended: **8 GB RAM or more**
- Multiplayer servers may need more depending on player count and view distance

For the best experience, keep the included resource packs enabled and avoid removing required datapacks unless you know exactly what they do.

### Credits and Disclaimer
CobbleStories is built on the work of many incredible Minecraft modders, datapack creators, artists, and resource pack developers.

Special thanks to the Cobblemon team, the Create team, and every creator whose work helps make this pack possible.

CobbleStories is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by Mojang, Microsoft, Nintendo, Creatures Inc., GAME FREAK, The Pokémon Company, or Cobbled Studios.


</details>
